headline: EagleTree Capital Acquires The Opus Group; Investment to Accelerate Global, Client-Centric Growth Across Opus Agency Brands
summary: NEW YORK, April 02, 2026--The Opus Group ("Opus"), a purpose-built network of agency brands delivering experiential, event, and brand marketing for more than 70 global companies, including some of the world's most influential brands, today announced that investment funds managed by EagleTree Capital ("EagleTree") have acquired Opus from its previous partner, Growth Catalyst Partners.

headline: FTC Crackdown: Buying Fake Reviews Now Carries $50K Fines for Businesses
summary: Buying fake 5-star reviews is now a federal crime carrying up to $51,744 in fines per violation. This crackdown by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has fundamentally changed the legal landscape for online marketing. According to a new Review Fraud Advisoryhttps://defamationalert.com/review-fraud-advisory issued today, business owners utilizing third-party services to boost their star ratings are now personally liable under Rule 16 CFR Part 465.

content.title: FTC Crackdown: Buying Fake Reviews Now Carries $50K Fines for Businesses
content.summary: Buying fake 5-star reviews is now a federal crime carrying up to $51,744 in fines per violation. This crackdown by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has fundamentally changed the legal landscape for online marketing. According to a new Review Fraud Advisoryhttps://defamationalert.com/review-fraud-advisory issued today, business owners utilizing third-party services to boost their star ratings are now personally liable under Rule 16 CFR Part 465.

headline: Tokenized Stocks Are Sweeping the Globe and Coming Soon to America
summary: While Hong Kong slept,  Leo Li  was buying Google.  In the early hours of March 25, Li bought a slug of digital tokens that represent about 6,940 shares of  Alphabet  Google’s corporate parent.  On this night, and most others, Li monitors the market from his Hong Kong home office, where he manages a traditional stock portfolio alongside a $6 million basket of tokens linked to shares of the Magnificent Seven and a gold fund.

content.title: Tokenized Stocks Are Sweeping the Globe and Coming Soon to America
content.summary: While Hong Kong slept,  Leo Li  was buying Google.
